---
title: "Macro Scenario Monitoring"
subtitle: "Vol. 1 | Before, during, and after the U.S.-Iran war"
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

<style>
.hero-grid,
.branch-grid,
.phase-grid,
.decision-grid {
  display: grid;
  gap: 1rem;
}

.hero-grid,
.branch-grid {
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr));
}

.phase-grid,
.decision-grid {
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(220px, 1fr));
}

.scenario-hero {
  display: grid;
  gap: 1rem;
}

.branch-card,
.phase-card,
.decision-card {
  padding: 1.1rem 1.15rem;
  border: 1px solid rgba(25, 48, 56, 0.1);
  border-radius: 1rem;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.72);
}

.branch-card h3,
.phase-card h3,
.decision-card h3 {
  margin: 0.2rem 0 0.65rem;
}

.branch-meta,
.hub-note {
  color: var(--muted);
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}

.branch-card ul,
.decision-card ul {
  margin: 0.6rem 0 0;
  padding-left: 1.05rem;
}

.branch-card li,
.decision-card li {
  margin: 0.35rem 0;
}

.branch-actions {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.7rem;
  margin-top: 0.95rem;
}
</style>

<div class="surface scenario-hero">
<p class="eyebrow">Decision, Risk and AI</p>
<h2>Macro Scenario Monitoring</h2>
<p>Vol. 1 studies one live geopolitical event through a sell-side macro lens. The organizing question is simple: how do we turn a before-war baseline, a live war shock, and post-war outcomes into a structured decision process using public data, explicit scenarios, and market confirmation signals?</p>
<p class="hub-note">This hub is intentionally simple. It introduces the event, the decision structure, and the two research branches. The detailed model, signal design, and index lists live inside each branch page.</p>
</div>

<div class="surface">
<p class="eyebrow">Vol. 1 frame</p>
<h3>One event, three phases</h3>
<div class="phase-grid">
<div class="phase-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Phase 1</p>
<h3>Pre-war baseline</h3>
<p>The benchmark world before the shock: growth, inflation, rates, and cross-asset pricing under a lower geopolitical premium.</p>
</div>
<div class="phase-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Phase 2</p>
<h3>Current war transmission</h3>
<p>The live shock: energy, shipping, food, inflation compensation, credit, and risk appetite are checked against explicit lagged market responses.</p>
</div>
<div class="phase-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Phase 3</p>
<h3>Post-war outcomes</h3>
<p>The two main paths after the conflict: relief and normalization, or persistent fragmentation with longer-lived inflation and risk premia.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="surface">
<p class="eyebrow">Research structure</p>
<h3>Two branches from the same scenario engine</h3>
<div class="branch-grid">
<div class="branch-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Branch A</p>
<h3>Commodity</h3>
<p>Energy and food are treated as physical markets first, then as macro transmission channels. This branch focuses on supply, storage, logistics, inventories, export flows, weather, and geopolitical risk premium.</p>
<p class="branch-meta">Goal: show how a commodity shock becomes an inflation, growth, FX, and country-risk story.</p>
<ul>
<li>Energy-centered decision model for oil, gas, products, and food spillovers.</li>
<li>Scenario design around pre-war, current war, relief, and persistent fragmentation.</li>
<li>Desk-style reading of EIA, IEA, OPEC, USDA, FAO, futures curves, and positioning.</li>
</ul>
<div class="branch-actions">
<a href="macro_scenario_commodity.html">Open commodity branch</a>
</div>
</div>
<div class="branch-card">
<p class="eyebrow">Branch B</p>
<h3>Investment</h3>
<p>This branch translates the same scenarios into sell-side macro forecasting and market interpretation. It follows the daily discipline of revising the growth, inflation, Fed, rates, FX, credit, and equity story for clients and internal desk partners.</p>
<p class="branch-meta">Goal: show how a macro economist updates scenarios, interprets signals, and turns them into a publishable desk note.</p>
<ul>
<li>Live scenario scoring using public signals and explicit lag logic.</li>
<li>Indicator playbook covering PMI, claims, payrolls, CPI/PCE, the yield curve, spreads, financial conditions, and GDP nowcasts.</li>
<li>Sell-side framing for forecast revision, sales/trading briefings, and client updates.</li>
</ul>
<div class="branch-actions">
<a href="macro_scenario_investment.html">Open investment branch</a>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="surface">
<p class="eyebrow">Decision model</p>
<h3>How the project is organized</h3>
<div class="decision-grid">
<div class="decision-card">
<h3>Scenario</h3>
<p>Each phase is translated into a base case and alternative scenarios with explicit reasoning, not only narrative judgment.</p>
</div>
<div class="decision-card">
<h3>Signals and indices</h3>
<p>Commodity reports, macro releases, and market signals are linked to each scenario through expected direction, lag, and transmission channel.</p>
</div>
<div class="decision-card">
<h3>Desk output</h3>
<p>The final output is a sell-side style research surface: what changed, what confirms it, what could invalidate it, and what clients should watch next.</p>
</div>
</div>
</div>
